campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss staffing challenges, including open positions and efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed housing security concerns and confirmed the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Housing security issues
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to approve financial reports and set up a public hearing process for an upcoming ordinance. The council also discussed whether the city should become a charter city and reviewed preliminary tax and revenue options.
keyTopics: Approved Engineer's Report for fiscal year finances
Public hearing scheduled for ordinance adoption (vote set for May 20)
Exploring charter city status—council to decide next steps
Reviewing potential tax options and revenue estimates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from recent sessions and authorize the Town Manager to finalize several contracts and agreements. The focus was on administrative approvals for software services, legal services, and other vendor agreements to keep town operations running.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from June 2 and June 8 council meetings
New five-year software services agreement
Updates to legal services contracts
Authorization for litigation legal services

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several major items including a proposal to allow ranked choice voting for mayoral and council vacancies, a long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America, and advertising signage at the airport. The council also considered designating a historic property at 647 South Sixth Street as a city landmark.
keyTopics: Ranked choice voting ballot measure for filling mayor and council vacancies
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America
Large format advertising sign contract at San José Mineta International Airport
Historic landmark designation and Mills Act contract for 647 South Sixth Street property

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council met to handle budget matters, celebrate community milestones, and address several ongoing city projects. The council discussed the upcoming fiscal year budget, water management plans, and construction projects around town, while also recognizing the Kiwanis Club's 100th anniversary and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to the judiciary.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits facing the city requiring legal counsel review
Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget and spending limits
Water shortage plans and urban water management updates
Public Safety Building construction project
Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving and South Bay Salt Pond restoration work

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. Closed sessions are private meetings where council discusses confidential matters like personnel, legal, or real estate issues that aren't open to the public.
keyTopics: Closed-session meeting held before regular council session
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
Specific agenda items for public discussion not detailed in this summary

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review and update the city's Health and Safety Element, which guides planning through 2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across multiple programs and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team achievement.
keyTopics: Updating Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Parks and Recreation Department recognition during July Parks and Recreation Month
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a hybrid meeting on June 30, 2026, allowing residents to attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city is continuing to offer multiple ways for the public to participate and submit comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation via Zoom at https://santaclaraca.zoom.us/j/99706759306
Phone dial-in option: 1(669) 900-6833
Written public comments can be submitted through eComment tab on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative and planning items, including updates to the city's five-year housing and community development plan, council member requests for future work projects, and a proposed change to how council vacancies are filled. The meeting also covered standard business like approving previous meeting minutes and scheduling upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (guides housing and federal funding priorities)
Council members' requests for new workplan items to address in coming months
Proposed ordinance to change the council vacancy process
Approval of previous meeting minutes and confirmation of council meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 10, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to handle routine business including scheduling and staff assignments. The main decision was approving a zoning change that reduces the required setback (building distance from the street) from the property line to 20 feet for a portion of Hansen Way at 3300 El Camino Real.
keyTopics: Zoning setback reduction at 3300 El Camino Real (Hansen Way)
Planning & Transportation Commission meeting minutes from April 29 approved
City Council schedule and staff assignment updates
